remember: Students will be able to recall the general methods of preparation of haloalkanes and haloarenes.
Students will be able to recall the physical and chemical properties of haloalkanes and haloarenes.
Students will be able to recall the reactions of haloalkanes and haloarenes such as substitution, elimination, and rearrangement reactions.
understand: Students will be able to explain the mechanisms of substitution and elimination reactions in haloalkanes.
Students will be able to describe the preparation and reactions of benzyl halides and vinyl halides.
Students will be able to understand the effect of substituents on the reactivity of haloarenes.
apply: Students will be able to apply the knowledge of synthesis and reactions of haloalkanes and haloarenes to solve problems.
Students will be able to predict the products of substitution and elimination reactions in haloalkanes.
Students will be able to explain the role of haloalkanes and haloarenes in various industrial and biological processes.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the factors affecting the rates of substitution and elimination reactions in haloalkanes.
Students will be able to evaluate the environmental impact of the production and use of haloalkanes and haloarenes.
Students will be able to compare the reactivity of different types of haloarenes.

ncert_objectives: Explain the general methods of preparation of haloalkanes and haloarenes, their physical and chemical properties, and the reactions of haloalkanes and haloarenes such as substitution, elimination, and rearrangement reactions.

Discuss the mechanisms of substitution and elimination reactions in haloalkanes, the preparation and reactions of benzyl halides and vinyl halides, and the effect of substituents on the reactivity of haloarenes.
board_exam_objectives: To identify the types of haloalkanes and haloarenes, their physical and chemical properties, and their reactions.
To explain the mechanisms of substitution and elimination reactions in haloalkanes and haloarenes.
To predict the products of substitution and elimination reactions in haloalkanes and haloarenes.
